pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 14, 22 confirms exact file path and naming convention
setup_task.sh lines 18-73 creates spec_data.json at this exact location
CSI MasterFormat standard (3-part specification structure): Part 1 - General, Part 2 - Products, Part 3 - Execution
CSI MasterFormat standard uses Heading 1 for PART section headers; task description specifies this style for PART 1, PART 2, PART 3
setup_task.sh line 27 specifies section_info.number as '09 30 13'; CSI 09 30 13 is the standard code for Ceramic Tiling specification section
setup_task.sh line 28 specifies section_info.title as 'Ceramic Tiling'
setup_task.sh line 21 includes 'Harborview Medical Center Expansion' as project name; Harborview Medical Center is a real hospital in Seattle, WA per UW Medicine official sources

setup_task.sh line 61 specifies 'Modified Dry-Set Cement Mortar: ANSI A118.4'; ANSI A118.4 is the real American National Standard Specifications for Modified Dry-Set Cement Mortar per TCNA and ANSI official sources
setup_task.sh line 44 specifies tile product mark 'T-1' with type 'Porcelain Floor Tile'
setup_task.sh line 52 specifies tile product mark 'T-2' with type 'Glazed Ceramic Wall Tile'
ODT format is ZIP-based; minimum valid ODT file is typically greater than 2000 bytes. This is a reasonable minimum threshold for a valid ODT document.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a CSI MasterFormat 3-part specification document (09 30 13 - Ceramic Tiling) for a synthetic project titled 'Harborview Medical Center Expansion' (though Harborview Medical Center itself is real, located in Seattle, WA). The spec must include PART 1 - General, PART 2 - Products, and PART 3 - Execution using Heading 1 styles. The document must reference industry standards including ANSI A118.4 (Modified Dry-Set Cement Mortar) and specify two tile products marked T-1 (Porcelain Floor Tile) and T-2 (Glazed Ceramic Wall Tile) with a tile schedule table. Output file is 093013_Ceramic_Tiling.odt, with minimum file size of 2000 bytes. All metadata claims correspond to synthetic data generated from the setup script, which includes realistic construction specification data.
